_comment: Edit this file to update the live tracker. After saving, the site auto-rebuilds in ~30s. JSON syntax matters — every string in double quotes, commas between items, no trailing commas. Paste into jsonlint.com if unsure. To add a new course/faculty/club: copy an existing block, paste, edit fields. The 'interest' field MUST exactly match one of the keys in the 'interests' array.

meta.title: Stanford GSB Prep — Class of 2028
meta.subtitle: Built for Alexis · Edit data.json on GitHub to update this page · Filter by interest, star what matters

courses.title: Clean Energy Opportunities
courses.why: Cornerstone non-technical energy class. Business models, tech change, policy. Right starting point.

courses.title: Clean Energy Project Development & Finance
courses.faculty: Dan Reicher, Dave Rogers, Jeffrey Brown
courses.why: Project structuring, financing, deployment. Closest to your VC/operator wheelhouse.

courses.title: Sustainable Energy: Business & Policy
courses.why: Pairs strategy with regulation — useful for emerging-markets work.

courses.title: Environmental Entrepreneurship
courses.why: Scaling sustainable businesses. Project-based.

courses.title: Energy & Environmental Policy Analysis
courses.why: Quant policy lens; cross-register if you want depth.

courses.title: Stanford Climate Ventures (3-quarter)
courses.why: Project sequence with engineers + scientists; real deployment problems.

courses.title: Understanding AI Technology for Business Problems
courses.why: Best non-technical AI class at GSB. Anthropic / OpenAI guests. Translate biz → AI solutions.

courses.title: The AI Awakening: Implications for Economy & Society
courses.why: Brynjolfsson is THE economist of AI. Heavy guest lectures.

courses.title: Digital Marketplaces & ML Applications
courses.why: Project-based. Best class for applying ML to real biz decisions.

courses.title: AI-Powered Org / Future of AI in Work
courses.why: Newer GSB AI Lab series. Multiple short modules — pick 1–2.

courses.title: AI@GSB workshops + Applied AI Initiative
courses.why: Not a course — but the most practical applied-AI prep at GSB. Sign up week 1.

courses.title: Venture Capital
courses.why: THE VC class. Term sheets, fund dynamics, deal mechanics. Do not skip.

courses.title: Formation of New Ventures
courses.why: Case-based with founders/CEOs. Prereq for STRAMGT 359.

courses.title: Aligning Startups With Their Markets
courses.why: Product-market fit deep dive. Rachleff = Wealthfront, Benchmark — exceptional teacher.

courses.title: Startup Garage
courses.why: GSB flagship hands-on. Build + test a startup with $1k stipend. Lottery — apply early.

courses.title: Lean LaunchPad
courses.why: Customer discovery / business model canvas. Cross-list — open to MBAs.

courses.title: Launch to Liquidity
courses.why: Scale → exit playbook. Strong for VC investors who want to coach founders.

courses.title: Entrepreneurship Without Borders / Emerging Markets
courses.why: EM-specific lens. Confirm offering — varies by year.

courses.title: Entrepreneurial Acquisitions
courses.faculty: Peter Kelly, Gerald Risk, Jim Ellis
courses.why: ETA / search fund mechanics. The class that built the model. Mandatory if interested.

courses.title: Managing Growing Enterprises (MGE)
courses.faculty: Peter Kelly, David Dodson, Coley Andrews, Kevin Taweel, Graham Weaver
courses.why: Founded by Irv Grousbeck — case-based scaling class. Multi-section. Other ETA must-take.

courses.title: Search Fund Garage / Practicum
courses.why: For serious post-MBA searchers. Apply after S543.

courses.title: Scaling Excellence
courses.why: Operational scaling — pairs with ETA stack.

courses.title: Marketing Management
courses.why: Distribution req in marketing. Foundation for any consumer play.

courses.title: Consumer Behavior / Frinky Science of the Mind
courses.why: Neuroeconomics of choice. Direct application to beauty branding (sensory + emotion).

courses.title: Brand & Storytelling courses
courses.why: Aaker's brand-personality framework underpins modern beauty positioning.

courses.title: Global eCommerce
courses.why: DTC + cross-border. Critical for any modern beauty brand.

courses.title: Digital Marketing Action Learning Program
courses.why: Project-based with a real brand client.

courses.title: Strategy in Technology Industries
courses.why: Burgelman is a legend — 40 years studying tech firm evolution. Foundational.

courses.title: The Industry / Industry of Industries
courses.why: Practitioner-led tech industry deep dives. Heavy case + guest based.

courses.title: Beyond Disruption / Innovation in Existing Orgs
courses.why: How incumbents innovate. Useful for search-fund-acquired companies.

courses.title: Platform Economics & Network Effects
courses.why: Marketplaces, ads, fintech. Best class for understanding modern tech moats.

courses.title: Technology Venture Formation / Tech Strategy
courses.why: Cross-register if you want engineering-flavored tech strategy.

courses.title: Leadership Labs
courses.why: Auto-enrolled. Take it seriously — feedback culture starts here.

courses.title: Interpersonal Dynamics ('Touchy Feely')
courses.why: Most-recommended class in GSB history. Apply. Apply. Apply.

courses.title: Paths to Power
courses.why: Pfeffer's signature. Two sections, ~68 each, lottery. Read his book before applying.

courses.title: Acting With Power
courses.why: Power through performance + behavioral psych. Pair with Paths to Power.

courses.title: Arbuckle Leadership Fellows (program)
courses.why: Apply Y1 spring to coach Y1s in Y2. Free executive-coach training.

courses.title: Negotiations / OB core electives
courses.faculty: Frank Flynn, Lindred Greer, Margaret Neale
courses.why: Neale's Negotiations is must-take in Y1 or Y2.

courses.title: Sports Business Management
courses.faculty: George Foster, S. Hinkie, D. Kaval
courses.why: GSB's sports class. Open to all MBAs. League/team/agent/college lenses.

courses.title: Datathletics: Data Analytics in Sports
courses.why: Tableau/SQL/Python for sports decisions. Light on prereqs.

courses.title: Sports Innovation Conference (March)
courses.why: Annual conference (13th in 2026). Volunteer to meet the speakers.

courses.title: Negotiations
courses.why: Neale is iconic. Useful for VC, ETA, and life.

courses.title: Global Management Immersion (GMIX)
courses.why: 4-week summer abroad. Use for EM VC connections (LatAm/Africa/SEA).

courses.title: Global Study Trips
courses.why: Short regional deep-dives. Cheap way to satisfy Global Experience Req.

faculty.name: Stefan Reichelstein
faculty.role: GSB · William R. Timken Professor (Emeritus, active)
faculty.teaches: Clean energy economics, carbon accounting, hydrogen, storage cost curves
faculty.why: Most accessible energy faculty for non-technical MBAs. Co-teaches Clean Energy Opportunities.

faculty.name: Dan Reicher
faculty.role: GSB / Law / Steyer-Taylor Center · former DOE Asst Secretary
faculty.teaches: Project finance, federal energy policy, climate venture
faculty.why: Best person for project finance + EM angle. Goes way back in clean energy.

faculty.name: Sally Benson
faculty.role: Doerr / Energy Sci & Eng · Precourt Family Professor
faculty.teaches: Carbon capture, geological storage, IPCC contributor
faculty.why: If you want to understand the technical realities of decarbonization at scale.

faculty.name: Arun Majumdar
faculty.why: Dean of Doerr; busy but engages with MBAs through Precourt events.

faculty.name: Erica Plambeck
faculty.why: Teaches Environmental Entrepreneurship. Approachable, action-oriented.

faculty.name: Jim Sweeney
faculty.why: Teaches MS&E energy policy class — open to MBAs.

faculty.name: Erik Brynjolfsson
faculty.role: GSB / HAI · Director Stanford Digital Economy Lab
faculty.teaches: Economics of AI, productivity, future of work
faculty.why: THE economist of generative AI. Heavy industry network.

faculty.name: Susan Athey
faculty.teaches: ML / causal inference applied to digital marketplaces
faculty.why: Best applied-ML teacher at GSB. Project-based pedagogy. National Academy member.

faculty.name: Fei-Fei Li
faculty.why: Hard to get time with, but HAI events and lectures are open. Aim for those.

faculty.name: Ilya Strebulaev
faculty.role: GSB · David S. Lobel Professor; Director, VC Initiative
faculty.teaches: Venture finance, fund dynamics, private market data
faculty.why: VC class instructor. EM-friendly — has worked on emerging market VC research.

faculty.name: Andy Rachleff
faculty.why: Maybe the best practitioner-teacher at GSB. Office hours fill fast.

faculty.name: Bill Barnett
faculty.teaches: Strategy, market dynamics, the 'Red Queen' framework
faculty.why: Co-teaches with Rachleff. Sharp strategic mind.

faculty.name: Hayagreeva 'Huggy' Rao
faculty.why: Author of Scaling Up Excellence + The Friction Project. Warm, generous.

faculty.name: Theresia Gouw
faculty.why: Co-teaches VC with Strebulaev. Useful network bridge.

faculty.name: Peter Kelly
faculty.role: GSB Lecturer · search fund operator (25+ acquisitions)
faculty.why: THE search fund teacher today. Office hours = priority access for searchers.

faculty.name: Irv Grousbeck
faculty.teaches: Operating, ownership, leadership at growing enterprises
faculty.why: Living legend of GSB ETA. Verify availability in 2026.

faculty.name: David Dodson
faculty.why: Dodson's MGE section is highly rated. Practical playbooks.

faculty.name: Graham Weaver
faculty.why: 2024 Distinguished Teaching Award. Inspiring.

faculty.name: Coley Andrews
faculty.role: GSB Lecturer · Co-founder Pacific Lake Partners
faculty.why: Investor side of ETA — meet if raising a search fund.

faculty.name: Jennifer Aaker
faculty.why: Foundational frameworks for any consumer brand builder.

faculty.name: Baba Shiv
faculty.why: His Frinky Science class is a cult favorite. Direct cosmetics relevance.

faculty.name: Pedro Gardete
faculty.why: Strong on DTC + targeted marketing.

faculty.name: Jonathan Levav
faculty.why: Award-winning teacher.

faculty.name: Robert Burgelman
faculty.teaches: Strategy in tech industries, corporate evolution
faculty.why: 200+ case studies. Old-school sharp.

faculty.name: Robert Siegel
faculty.why: Practitioner who runs 'The Industry' — pulls in great guests.

faculty.name: Charles O'Reilly
faculty.role: GSB · Frank E. Buck Professor (Emeritus, active)
faculty.teaches: Org culture, ambidexterity, innovation in incumbents
faculty.why: Lifetime Achievement Award. Direct relevance to ETA-acquired companies.

faculty.name: Bob Sutton
faculty.role: Engineering / GSB courtesy · Center for Work, Tech, Org
faculty.why: Author 'Good Boss, Bad Boss', 'No Asshole Rule'. Approachable.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Pfeffer
faculty.role: GSB · Thomas D. Dee II Professor
faculty.why: Read 'Power' and '7 Rules of Power' before approaching. Blunt and brilliant.

faculty.name: Deborah Gruenfeld
faculty.why: Acting With Power instructor. Pair with Pfeffer.

faculty.name: Frank Flynn
faculty.role: GSB · Paul E. Holden Professor of OB
faculty.why: Often teaches the OB core. Strong researcher + teacher.

faculty.name: Margaret Neale
faculty.why: Negotiations is a must-take. Neale is THE name.

faculty.name: George Foster
faculty.role: GSB · Konosuke Matsushita Professor; Sports Mgmt Initiative
faculty.why: Runs the only formal sports business class. Connects to Lacob/Ballmer alumni network.

faculty.name: Roger Noll
faculty.why: Less course-active but unmissable for sports econ context.

roadmap.body: Read Search Fund Primer (CES); Pfeffer's 'Power'; Brynjolfsson's 'AI Awakening' essay; rough draft of your goals doc.
roadmap.notes: Reach out to CES + Doerr to introduce yourself. Connect with 3–5 Class of 2027 students in your interest areas.

roadmap.body: Year 1 Core (Accounting, OB, Strategy, etc.) + OB 374 Leadership Labs (mandatory) + Negotiations if available.
roadmap.notes: Don't pile on electives Y1 fall — settle in. Find 2–3 peer advisors. Sign up for Stanford Energy Club + Sports Mgmt Club.

roadmap.body: Year 1 Core finishing + 1 elective from VC (FINANCE 384) OR Brynjolfsson's AI Awakening.
roadmap.notes: Apply to Arbuckle Leadership Fellows in spring.

roadmap.body: Formation of New Ventures (STRAMGT 353); Lean LaunchPad (ENGR 245); apply to Touchy Feely lottery.
roadmap.notes: Decide: GMIX summer? Internship? Search trip? Confirm Global Experience plan.

roadmap.body: Internship OR GMIX OR independent search-fund prep / energy project.
roadmap.notes: If energy: shadow a project at a developer. If search: 8 weeks of prospecting + LP outreach.

roadmap.body: Touchy Feely (if won lottery); S543 Entrepreneurial Acquisitions; Clean Energy Project Dev & Finance (Reicher); Burgelman Strategy in Tech.
roadmap.notes: Heaviest quarter. Apply for Paths to Power for winter.

roadmap.body: FINANCE 384 Venture Capital (if not done); Susan Athey ML/Marketplaces; OR Pfeffer Paths to Power; Clean Energy Opportunities (Reichelstein).
roadmap.notes: Run a small AI pilot in your Startup Garage / search target this quarter.

roadmap.body: Acting With Power (Gruenfeld); S549 Search Fund Garage OR Environmental Entrepreneurship; Sports Business Mgmt (curiosity slot).
roadmap.notes: Last chance for high-touch electives. Don't waste it on filler.

roadmap.body: Search fund / energy project / VC partnership.
roadmap.notes: Use CES + Doerr alumni networks aggressively. They're built for this.

advisorQs.q: What's the realistic course load per quarter? When do most people front-load vs. back-load?
Which Distribution Requirement classes are over-subscribed and which can I take any quarter?
Which electives lose 50%+ of applicants? When should I apply for Touchy Feely / Paths to Power / Acting With Power?
Walk me through course bidding. How many points do I get? Is bidding strategy a thing here?
Realistically, can I add the MS Environment & Resources (E-IPER) joint degree after I arrive, or do I need to apply now?
Is the new GSB Applied AI initiative considered a 'certificate'? What does formal participation give me?
What's the standard prep timeline for someone planning to launch a search fund right after graduation?
advisorQs.who: Peter Kelly / CES Search Fund team

advisorQs.q: I'm a non-technical investor interested in project development. Should I prioritize Doerr classes or GSB energy electives?
advisorQs.who: Dan Reicher / Precourt MBA contact / CES energy advisor

advisorQs.q: What classes touch emerging market VC, and which professors actively research it?
Are there GSB faculty/lecturers from beauty, or alumni who'd guest-lecture? Who runs Retail & Luxury Club?
Beyond GSBGEN 360, is there a way to do a Stanford Athletics-side independent study or project?
Which Global Study Trips are best for VC + emerging markets? (LatAm? Africa? SEA?)
Can I be assigned a career advisor who specializes in (a) VC, (b) ETA, and (c) climate tech? Or do I have to pick one?
What's the etiquette for cold-emailing a faculty member I haven't taken a class with? Lunch programs?
Can I run an independent study for credit, e.g. building an AI use case for a small business I'm scoping for ETA?
advisorQs.who: Peter Kelly / Susan Athey / dept chair

advisorQs.q: Can I audit classes I didn't get into the lottery for?
How much time off campus is realistic in Y2 (board roles, deal-sourcing, beauty brand consulting)?

resources.name: Center for Entrepreneurial Studies (CES)
resources.why: Home base for VC, entrepreneurship, search fund. Curates 50+ courses. Office hours, mentor pool.

resources.name: Venture Capital Initiative (Strebulaev)
resources.why: VC research, data, events. Get on the mailing list day 1.

resources.name: Search Fund Primer + 2024 Search Fund Study
resources.why: GSB's official ETA bibles. Read both before fall.

resources.name: Precourt Institute for Energy
resources.why: Energy seminars, fellowships, sustainable finance initiative. Cross-school.

resources.name: Doerr School of Sustainability
resources.why: Cross-register here for technical energy/climate classes.

resources.name: Steyer-Taylor Center for Energy Policy & Finance
Stanford HAI
resources.why: Best AI events on campus. Open to MBAs.

resources.name: AI@GSB / Applied AI Initiative
resources.why: Hands-on AI workshops, practitioner sessions. Your fast track to applied AI.

resources.name: GSB Sports Management Initiative
resources.why: George Foster's research + cases. Engages with Lacob/Ballmer alumni.

resources.name: Stanford Energy Club
resources.why: Speaker series, treks (e.g. geothermal in Iceland). Join Y1 fall.

resources.name: GSB Consumer & Retail Club / Luxury Trek
resources.why: Luxury Trek visits LVMH, Cartier, prestige brands in Europe.

resources.name: Sports Management Club (GSB)
resources.why: Treks to teams + leagues. Hosts SIC.

resources.name: Arbuckle Leadership Fellows Program
resources.why: Apply Y1 spring. Coach Y1s in Y2. Built-in executive-coach training.

resources.name: MS Environment & Resources (E-IPER) / MBA
resources.why: If energy is your THING, evaluate this in Y1 fall.

resources.name: GMIX (Global Management Immersion)
resources.why: 4 weeks abroad — perfect for EM VC connections.

resources.name: Explore Courses
resources.why: The actual source of truth for course offerings each quarter.

resources.name: GSB Academic Calendar
resources.why: Bidding/registration windows. Bookmark and live by it.

clubs.name: Africa Business Club
clubs.why: Most direct on-ramp into the Africa-focused community at GSB. Speaker series, treks (Lagos, Nairobi, Cape Town), case discussions. Bridges to Stanford Seed.

clubs.name: Latin American Student Association (LASA)
clubs.why: Speaker series, career events, social glue for Latam community at GSB. Useful if you ever want to source/deploy in LatAm.

clubs.name: GSB Energy Club
clubs.why: Speaker series on grid, electrification, decarbonization, climate tech. Treks. Often the first place energy career intel lands.

clubs.name: Stanford Energy Club (cross-campus)
clubs.why: Bigger umbrella across Stanford — engineers, scientists, MBAs. Worth being a member of both this and the GSB-only club.

clubs.name: GSB VC Club
clubs.why: Pitch competitions, VC firm visits, panels with practitioners. Apply to leadership team in Y1 spring if you want a louder voice.

clubs.name: GSB AI Club
clubs.why: Bridges frontier AI development to business applications. Hosts builders from frontier labs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.).

clubs.name: Entrepreneur Club (E-Club)
clubs.why: First stop for any entrepreneurial interest at GSB. Founders' lunches, idea labs, demo days.

clubs.name: Search Fund / ETA community at CES
clubs.why: Not a formal 'club' but a tight community via CES. Search fund dinners, alumni searcher visits, mentor matching.

clubs.name: Consumer & Retail Club / Luxury Trek
clubs.why: Luxury Trek (Europe) visits LVMH, Cartier, prestige beauty. CPG and beauty exec speakers throughout the year.

clubs.name: High Tech Club
clubs.why: Tech career fair, recruiter events, treks to Bay Area tech companies. Massive — most popular career-track club.

clubs.name: Sports Management Club (GSB)
clubs.why: Annual Sports Innovation Conference, treks to teams + leagues, owner/exec speakers (Lacob, Ballmer network).

clubs.name: Stanford Sports Analytics Club (cross-campus)
clubs.why: Quant side of sports — analytics talks, datathons. Worth checking out for fun even if not your career.

clubs.name: Investment Management Club (Public Markets)
clubs.why: More public markets than VC, but solid speaker series and useful for understanding capital flows broadly.

clubs.name: Private Equity Club
clubs.why: Adjacent to ETA — many search funders engage. PE recruiting events too.

clubs.name: Real Estate Club
clubs.why: Useful if you ever look at infrastructure / RE-adjacent deals (data centers, solar farms, etc.).

clubs.name: Stanford Women in Business (SWIB)
clubs.why: Speaker series, mentorship, career programming. Open to all genders as allies.
